mentions.note: Core defensive holding in the charitable trust providing cushion during today's tech meltdown. Acts as a 'permit' enabling safe ownership of riskier growth plays. Doesn't boom but doesn't crash either.
Best-of-breed consumer staples leader with long history of dividend growth, great brands, strong balance sheet; exemplifies quality worth paying premium for.
Owned by charitable trust; tempted to buy more but no reason to believe rebound coming; no bottom in sight.
New CEO; expect weak quarter given recent weak trends; too soon for turnaround; like stock as slowdown hedge; cheap valuation; own in charitable trust.
Consumer staple winner in February; outperformed tech/hardware due to defensive characteristics.
Software buyer rallying on premise of automation savings; had weak quarter but stock up; benefits from software spending and efficiency gains.

Nearly half of sales overseas; weak dollar tailwind is huge positive. Recent pullback in stock despite disappointing quarter likely due to currency benefit expectations — exporter upside from USD weakness.
Rallied on what many thought was bad quarter; provides positive precedent for similar companies.
Weak quarter met crushed expectations; new CEO Shilesh Dejurikar shows momentum and international growth insight (Brazil +8%, China +3%); rallied 3% on psychology shift; buy on any pullback.
New CEO, great brands, 3% yield, stock down significantly; own for bounce despite weak quarter expected; report Thursday.
Defensive hedge against weak economy; up today on recession fears; owns toothpaste, needs no matter what; good defensive hedge until clarity.
Dividend aristocrat (69 years), 3% yield, tumbled from $180; buy in tranches on further weakness; quality franchise on sale.
